description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.
An artificial eye. Choose Kiroshi Optics!
Must be paired. Provides immunity to dazzling effects (like flashbangs).
A base interface for audio enhancements.
Provides immunity to deafness or sudden loud noises.
Built in agent. Audio only but can be linked with other cybergear.
Deals no damage. If hit roll DV15 Resist Torture/Drugs. On a fail take the Damaged Eye and Damaged Ear Critical injuries (see pg. 188) for 1 minute. No Crit damage dealt.
A heavy SMG. Due to its size it can't be concealed. +1 to Attack.
Turns weapon into a "Smartgun". Refer to all rules specified on pg. 344 before using.
Reduces penalties against targets in darkness, smoke/fog to 0.
Double ablation to armor.
A very heavy pistol. It can't be concealed.
Generic Light Melee weapon.
Medium version of an Armorjack. -2 to REF, DEX, and MOVE.
Medium version of an Armorjack for your head. -2 to REF, DEX, and MOVE.
Access to nervous system.
Plugs in the wrist or head to allow you to connect to machines/systems hands-free.
Speedware. Permanent +2 to Initiative.
Provides +2 to Resist Torture/Drugs checks.
Provides immunity to inhalable gases or fumes.
Doubles natural healing while resting.
Basic ammo, nothing special.

A basic lob or pitch of an object. Use an Action to make a Ranged Attack at up to 25m/yds. Targets can choose to Dodge your throw if they can Dodge bullets. Melee weapons deal their base damage, but don't penetrate half SP.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 4/Elite Threat - Mid-level, named threats, who run the bulk of activity for most orgs within NC. If a PC didn't choose to become an edgerunner, they probably would've eventually ended up in a position like this instead.

Ability - Threat Detection: When entering the scene, the security officers immediately identify the most major potential threat (up to two targets) via context clues and patient profiles, immediately tagging them as such. They gain a +2 to checks made against those targets.

Corp-Wide Ability - Trauma Response: When a Trauma Team agent takes an action, any other agent which takes that same action towards a same or similar target/goal in that initiative round receives +2 to their check. This effect stacks.

Heavily armed and armored, Trauma Team’s arrival tends to lead to ceasefires, even within the bloodiest conflicts, as anyone who continues fighting is seen as an aggressor and dealt with accordingly. Rumors of “Trauma Diplomacy” - that just the appearance of TT leads to the parties talking it out - have likely been greatly exaggerated, though. Violence will likely resume as soon as the transport leaves. For more details about their arrival times and formations, players can see CORE p. 223-224.

You ever wanted to feel like you’re helping people, even though your primary skillset comes from the barrel of a gun? Well, Trauma Team’s always hiring for grunts with guns - all you have to do is indebit yourself to the corp for the tech they force you to use! Just be careful on the job, if it’s a choice between extracting you and extracting the client and docs safely, they pick the client every time.
